judge.rationale: This implementation is severe architectural slop that completely fails to implement the required functionality. It imports a non-existent 'module_' (which would cause an ImportError at runtime), contains no actual iter_file_chunks function, and instead consists entirely of a bizarre meta-programming shim that tries to alias Dafny-compiled symbols into the global namespace. There is no file reading, no chunk yielding, no validation of chunk_size, and no iterator logic whatsoever. The code is maximally indirect, opaque, and non-functional — the antithesis of the Zen of Python's principles of simplicity, explicitness, and readability.
